title: gcp-sm: GCP_ACCESS_TOKEN expires after 1h; no refresh mechanism
description: Scenario: a long-running daemon constructs GcpSmBackend at startup using a token from 'gcloud auth print-access-token'. After 1 hour, every get() and put() fails with HTTP 401 mapped to SecretError::Backend (permanent). The daemon has no way to recover without restart — because from_uri reads the token once and stores it for the object's lifetime. The module doc acknowledges this but relegates the production guidance (Workload Identity / a refreshing SDK) to a link. Maintenance consequence: users of short-lived scripts are fine; daemon users hit this in production. This should be flagged much more prominently — at minimum a warning in the struct doc and a note in from_uri's doc. Longer-term: consider a callback or environment-variable-reread on 401.
